scope.context_note: Volume II section from an undergraduate real analysis note set. The local mathematical setting is chapter 'Constructing the Whole Numbers', section 'Algebraic Structure Of $\mathbb{W}$'. The audit goal is in-depth learning of the structure: direct dependencies should be concepts needed to state each node precisely, with common ambient structure carried by the nearest appropriate ancestor.

review_instructions.order: Nodes are topologically sorted so dependencies and ambient ancestors appear before their users when possible.
review_instructions.state: Use reorder/state/working-delta.json as cumulative accepted state across all batches.
review_instructions.checker: Run reorder/tools/check_dependency_edge.py before accepting any add/remove.

nodes.title: $\mathbb{N}$ Embeds into $\mathbb{W}$ as the Positive Part
nodes.statement_display:
The inclusion map identifies $\mathbb{N}$ with the positive part
$\mathbb{W}\setminus\{0\}$ and preserves successor, $1$, addition,
multiplication, and order.
nodes.statement_tex:
The inclusion map identifies $\mathbb{N}$ with the positive part
$\mathbb{W}\setminus\{0\}$ and preserves successor, $1$, addition,
multiplication, and order.

direct_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{W}$ Extends Order on $\mathbb{N}$
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\leq_{\mathbb{W}}b\Longleftrightarrow a\leq_{\mathbb{N}}b, \qquad a<_{\mathbb{W}}b\Longleftrightarrow a<_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursive Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. A function $f:P\to W$ is called the \emph{general recursive function determined by $(W,c,\Phi)$} exactly when \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Less Than or Equal on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a \le b \] exactly when \[ a<b \qquad\text{or}\qquad a=b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ g_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ g_m(1)=m \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(g_m(S(n))=g_m(n)+m). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m \cdot n \coloneqq g_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=0,\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}0=0, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b=a\cdot_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $\leq_{\mathbb{W}}$ on $\mathbb{W}$ by declaring \[ 0\leq_{\mathbb{W}}a\quad(a\in\mathbb{W}), \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\leq_{\mathbb{W}}b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a\leq_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Stage-Dependent Step Rule
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system and let $W$ be a set. A \emph{stage-dependent step rule} on $W$ over $P$ is a function \[ \Phi:P\times W\to W. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Less Than on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a<b \] exactly when there exists $c \in P$ such that \[ b=a+c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Order on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $<_{\mathbb{W}}$ on $\mathbb{W}$ by declaring \[ 0<_{\mathbb{W}}n\quad(n\in\mathbb{N}), \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a<_{\mathbb{W}}b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a<_{\mathbb{N}}b. \] No element of $\mathbb{W}$ is strictly less than $0$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$ Extends Addition on $\mathbb{N}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursion by State Encoding
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. There exists a function $H:P\to P\times W$ such that \[ H(1)=(1,c) \] and \[ \forall n\in P\; \bigl(H(S(n))=(S(\pi_1(H(n))),\Phi(\pi_1(H(n)),\pi_2(H(n))))\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$ Extends Multiplication on $\mathbb{N}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b=a\cdot_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{W}$ Extends Order on $\mathbb{N}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\leq_{\mathbb{W}}b\Longleftrightarrow a\leq_{\mathbb{N}}b, \qquad a<_{\mathbb{W}}b\Longleftrightarrow a<_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

dependencies.title: Multiplication on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ g_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ g_m(1)=m \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(g_m(S(n))=g_m(n)+m). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m \cdot n \coloneqq g_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: Iterator Data
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: General Recursive Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. A function $f:P\to W$ is called the \emph{general recursive function determined by $(W,c,\Phi)$} exactly when \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Stage-Dependent Step Rule
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system and let $W$ be a set. A \emph{stage-dependent step rule} on $W$ over $P$ is a function \[ \Phi:P\times W\to W. \]

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

dependencies.title: General Recursion by State Encoding
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. ancestor_tree.title: Order on $\mathbb{W}$ Extends Order on $\mathbb{N}$
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\leq_{\mathbb{W}}b\Longleftrightarrow a\leq_{\mathbb{N}}b, \qquad a<_{\mathbb{W}}b\Longleftrightarrow a<_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{W}$

dependencies.title: Less Than or Equal on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a \le b \] exactly when \[ a<b \qquad\text{or}\qquad a=b. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

nodes.title: $(\mathbb{W},+,0)$ Is a Commutative Monoid
nodes.statement_display: The structure $(\mathbb{W},+_{\mathbb{W}},0)$ is a commutative monoid.
nodes.statement_tex: The structure $(\mathbb{W},+_{\mathbb{W}},0)$ is a commutative monoid.

direct_dependencies.title: Zero Is an Additive Identity on $\mathbb{W}$

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a+b=b+a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b,c\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ (a+_{\mathbb{W}}b)+_{\mathbb{W}}c = a+_{\mathbb{W}}(b+_{\mathbb{W}}c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=b+_{\mathbb{W}}a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Plus $n$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ 1+n=S(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is an Additive Identity on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $a\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a \qquad\text{and}\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a. \]

dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation

A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Less Than or Equal on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a \le b \] exactly when \[ a<b \qquad\text{or}\qquad a=b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{W}$

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: No Natural Number Equals Itself Plus a Natural Number
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ n \neq m+n. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: No Object Is Its Own Successor
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ S(n) \neq n. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is the Least Element of $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $a\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ 0\leq_{\mathbb{W}}a. \]

ancestor_tree.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Less Than or Equal on a Peano System

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Language
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

nodes.title: $(\mathbb{W},\cdot,1)$ Is a Commutative Monoid
nodes.statement_display: The structure $(\mathbb{W},\cdot_{\mathbb{W}},1)$ is a commutative monoid.
nodes.statement_tex: The structure $(\mathbb{W},\cdot_{\mathbb{W}},1)$ is a commutative monoid.

direct_dependencies.title: One Is a Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{W}$

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursive Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. A function $f:P\to W$ is called the \emph{general recursive function determined by $(W,c,\Phi)$} exactly when \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ g_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ g_m(1)=m \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(g_m(S(n))=g_m(n)+m). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m \cdot n \coloneqq g_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=0,\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}0=0, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b=a\cdot_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Stage-Dependent Step Rule
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system and let $W$ be a set. A \emph{stage-dependent step rule} on $W$ over $P$ is a function \[ \Phi:P\times W\to W. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a+b=b+a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursion by State Encoding
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. There exists a function $H:P\to P\times W$ such that \[ H(1)=(1,c) \] and \[ \forall n\in P\; \bigl(H(S(n))=(S(\pi_1(H(n))),\Phi(\pi_1(H(n)),\pi_2(H(n))))\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a \cdot b) \cdot c = a \cdot (b \cdot c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a \cdot b = b \cdot a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Distributes Over Addition
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ a \cdot (b+c) = (a \cdot b) + (a \cdot c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b,c\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ (a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b)\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}c = a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}(b\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b=b\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Successor on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m \cdot S(n) = (m \cdot n) + m. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication With $1$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m \cdot 1 = m. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Is a Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $a\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ 1\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=a \qquad\text{and}\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}1=a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Plus $n$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ 1+n=S(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Times $n$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ 1 \cdot n = n. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is Absorbing for Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $a\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ 0\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=0 \qquad\text{and}\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}0=0. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a+b=b+a. \]

dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Axiom

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: General Recursion by State Encoding
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. There exists a function $H:P\to P\times W$ such that \[ H(1)=(1,c) \] and \[ \forall n\in P\; \bigl(H(S(n))=(S(\pi_1(H(n))),\Phi(\pi_1(H(n)),\pi_2(H(n))))\bigr). \]

dependencies.title: One Times $n$
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ 1 \cdot n = n. \]

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: Multiplication on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ g_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ g_m(1)=m \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(g_m(S(n))=g_m(n)+m). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m \cdot n \coloneqq g_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: General Recursion by State Encoding
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. There exists a function $H:P\to P\times W$ such that \[ H(1)=(1,c) \] and \[ \forall n\in P\; \bigl(H(S(n))=(S(\pi_1(H(n))),\Phi(\pi_1(H(n)),\pi_2(H(n))))\bigr). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m \cdot 1 = m. \]

direct_dependencies.title: $(\mathbb{W},+,0)$ Is a Commutative Monoid
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{W},+_{\mathbb{W}},0)$ is a commutative monoid.

direct_dependencies.title: $(\mathbb{W},\cdot,1)$ Is a Commutative Monoid
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{W},\cdot_{\mathbb{W}},1)$ is a commutative monoid.

direct_dependencies.title: Zero Is Absorbing for Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursive Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. A function $f:P\to W$ is called the \emph{general recursive function determined by $(W,c,\Phi)$} exactly when \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ g_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ g_m(1)=m \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(g_m(S(n))=g_m(n)+m). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m \cdot n \coloneqq g_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=0,\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}0=0, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b=a\cdot_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Stage-Dependent Step Rule
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system and let $W$ be a set. A \emph{stage-dependent step rule} on $W$ over $P$ is a function \[ \Phi:P\times W\to W. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a+b=b+a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b,c\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ (a+_{\mathbb{W}}b)+_{\mathbb{W}}c = a+_{\mathbb{W}}(b+_{\mathbb{W}}c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=b+_{\mathbb{W}}a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursion by State Encoding
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. There exists a function $H:P\to P\times W$ such that \[ H(1)=(1,c) \] and \[ \forall n\in P\; \bigl(H(S(n))=(S(\pi_1(H(n))),\Phi(\pi_1(H(n)),\pi_2(H(n))))\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a \cdot b) \cdot c = a \cdot (b \cdot c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a \cdot b = b \cdot a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Distributes Over Addition
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ a \cdot (b+c) = (a \cdot b) + (a \cdot c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Distributes over Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b,c\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}(b+_{\mathbb{W}}c) = (a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b)+_{\mathbb{W}}(a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b,c\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ (a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b)\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}c = a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}(b\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b=b\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Successor on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m \cdot S(n) = (m \cdot n) + m. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication With $1$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m \cdot 1 = m. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Is a Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $a\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ 1\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=a \qquad\text{and}\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}1=a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Plus $n$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ 1+n=S(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Times $n$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ 1 \cdot n = n. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: $(\mathbb{W},+,0)$ Is a Commutative Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{W},+_{\mathbb{W}},0)$ is a commutative monoid.

transitive_dependencies.title: $(\mathbb{W},\cdot,1)$ Is a Commutative Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{W},\cdot_{\mathbb{W}},1)$ is a commutative monoid.

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is Absorbing for Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $a\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ 0\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=0 \qquad\text{and}\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}0=0. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is an Additive Identity on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $a\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a \qquad\text{and}\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a. \]

dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Peano System

dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: Iterator Data
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: General Recursive Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. A function $f:P\to W$ is called the \emph{general recursive function determined by $(W,c,\Phi)$} exactly when \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Stage-Dependent Step Rule

dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: General Recursion by State Encoding
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. There exists a function $H:P\to P\times W$ such that \[ H(1)=(1,c) \] and \[ \forall n\in P\; \bigl(H(S(n))=(S(\pi_1(H(n))),\Phi(\pi_1(H(n)),\pi_2(H(n))))\bigr). \]

dependencies.title: Addition Is Commutative
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a+b=b+a. \]

dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition With $1$

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: One Is a Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{W}$
dependencies.statement_preview: For every $a\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ 1\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=a \qquad\text{and}\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}1=a. \]

dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$
dependencies.statement_preview: Define $\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=0,\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}0=0, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b=a\cdot_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: General Recursion by State Encoding
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. There exists a function $H:P\to P\times W$ such that \[ H(1)=(1,c) \] and \[ \forall n\in P\; \bigl(H(S(n))=(S(\pi_1(H(n))),\Phi(\pi_1(H(n)),\pi_2(H(n))))\bigr). \]

dependencies.title: Multiplication Successor on the Right
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m \cdot S(n) = (m \cdot n) + m. \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: General Recursive Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. A function $f:P\to W$ is called the \emph{general recursive function determined by $(W,c,\Phi)$} exactly when \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

dependencies.title: General Recursion by State Encoding
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. There exists a function $H:P\to P\times W$ such that \[ H(1)=(1,c) \] and \[ \forall n\in P\; \bigl(H(S(n))=(S(\pi_1(H(n))),\Phi(\pi_1(H(n)),\pi_2(H(n))))\bigr). \]

dependencies.title: Stage-Dependent Step Rule
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system and let $W$ be a set. A \emph{stage-dependent step rule} on $W$ over $P$ is a function \[ \Phi:P\times W\to W. \]

nodes.title: $\mathbb{W}$ Is a Commutative Ordered Semiring
nodes.statement_display:
With the operations $+_{\mathbb{W}}$ and $\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}$ and the order
$\le_{\mathbb{W}}$, the whole numbers form a commutative ordered semiring.
nodes.statement_tex:
With the operations $+_{\mathbb{W}}$ and $\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}$ and the order
$\le_{\mathbb{W}}$, the whole numbers form a commutative ordered semiring.

direct_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{W}$ Extends Order on $\mathbb{N}$
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\leq_{\mathbb{W}}b\Longleftrightarrow a\leq_{\mathbb{N}}b, \qquad a<_{\mathbb{W}}b\Longleftrightarrow a<_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

direct_dependencies.title: $(\mathbb{W},+,\cdot,0,1)$ Is a Commutative Semiring
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{W},+_{\mathbb{W}},\cdot_{\mathbb{W}},0,1)$ is a commutative semiring.
